header_summary:
The latest update of the developer community's project, Backbone, marks its 6th release, significantly enhancing the quality assurance and multi-daemon process testing. It successfully integrates bitcoind's Python functional tests, demonstrating improved initial setup and shutdown processes across multiple daemons. Developers can access the updated code at [bitcoinbackbone.org](http://bitcoinbackbone.org) and explore further by using the demo provided for Linux systems [source URL](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CALZpt+Ec=cmFmEesnQ4DKvux-aK80ZiMw30+N8ZGyxDU2tWvxg@mail.gmail.com/T/#u#mfbfd449a00f35bb3e6e5b78a1860efd8979a6379).

Bitcoin Core's new version 30.3 introduces various enhancements including bug fixes, performance improvements, and additional features, aiming to optimize the user experience across multiple operating systems. It is available for download with detailed upgrade instructions at [BitcoinCore.org](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-30.3/), and users are encouraged to report issues via [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues). This release also supports a wide range of developers contributing to the ongoing maintenance and enhancement of the software [source URL](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/c359b0af-ea71-4a8c-913a-da0e65b8aa29n@googlegroups.com/T/#u#md54f0ba29b583ece2d476cb9117024646b3661cd).

In addition to Bitcoin Core updates, there are advanced security discussions focused on enhancing cryptocurrency transaction security and recovery mechanisms. Various recovery schemes have been proposed, including Xpub derivation-based recovery for BIP 32 based wallets and stateful timestamped proofs to protect against potential quantum computing threats. These efforts aim for comprehensive recovery across all network address types, enhancing the security of cryptocurrency transactions and ownership [source URL](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/post-quantum-recovery-of-hashed-addresses-with-no-confiscatory-risk/2714/2).

Lastly, the release of Bitcoin Inquisition version 29.4 introduces several consensus changes to improve transaction functionality and security. Notable updates include support for BIP 118 and BIP 119, among others, progressively activated on the default signet. These changes can be explored further and downloaded from their [official GitHub repository](https://github.com/bitcoin-inquisition/bitcoin/releases/tag/v29.4-inq), enhancing transaction security and flexibility within the Bitcoin network [source URL](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/bitcoin-inquisition-29-4/2739).

recent_posts.title: new backbone code release with bitcoind python test framework integration
recent_posts.summary:
- The Backbone project's 6th release improves quality assurance and integrates bitcoind tests.
- Enhanced backbone-cli controls and internal CLI command optimization are underway.
- Ongoing enhancements focus on peer-to-peer coverage and utilizing cryptographic experiments.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core 30.3 released
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version 30.3 features new enhancements and bug fixes.
- It supports Linux Kernel 3.17+, macOS 13+, and Windows 10+.
- Significant changes include a fix to reduce disk operations in the chainstate database.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core 29.4 released
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version 29.4 offers new fixes and system enhancements.
- Users must shut down previous versions fully before installing upgrades.
- The software's compatibility includes Linux, macOS 13+, and Windows 10+.

recent_posts.title: Post Quantum Recovery Of Hashed Addresses With No Confiscatory Risk
recent_posts.summary:
- The email discusses enhancing security for cryptocurrency transactions and recovery methods.
- Proposed recovery schemes include new encumbrances and commit-reveal strategies.
- The sender seeks feedback on the proposal for comprehensive recovery mechanisms.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Inquisition 29.4
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Inquisition, version 29.4, is based on Bitcoin Core 29.4 update.
- It integrates consensus changes like BIP 118 and BIP 119 for improved security.
- Significant new operations include OP_CAT, OP_CHECKSIGFROMSTACK, and OP_INTERNALKEY.

recent_posts.title: RFC: Block-Range Filters (a.k.a. Hierarchical filters)
recent_posts.summary:
- Advanced data structures improve set membership query efficiency and adaptability.
- Challenges like transaction ID collisions necessitate designs with lower false positive rates.
- Practical applications include rapid synchronization in servers using structured data queries.

active_posts.title: Quantum Recovery Of Hashed Address Secured Coins With No Confiscatory Risk
active_posts.summary:
- Quantum-safe signature schemes are essential for cryptocurrency security in a quantum world.
- Various proposals, like hierarchical and stateful timestamped proofs, address recovery challenges.
- Non-hashed address types are still vulnerable to quantum attacks despite new recovery methods.

active_posts.title: BIP draft: CISA for Taproot Key Path Spends
active_posts.summary: - A Bitcoin Improvement Proposal draft introduces cross-input signature aggregation.  - BIP 458 and 459 detail half-aggregation and full-aggregation, respectively.  - Input for this proposal is available online, with provisions for community feedback.

active_posts.title: The game-theory problems of PQ sunsetting modes
active_posts.summary: - Quantum strategies may impact Bitcoin's security and economic dynamics.  - Miner and quantum computing collusion could extract billions from Bitcoin.  - New defensive mechanisms are essential for blockchain stability in a post-quantum era.

active_posts.title: Addressing the Diminishing Block Subsidy
active_posts.summary:
- A proposed 0.25 BTC tail emission per block aims to stabilize miner revenue by 2040.
- Critics fear the new model may weaken Bitcoin's value narrative by increasing supply.
- Flexible adjustments to Bitcoin's emission rate will cater to future network security needs.

active_posts.title: P2share: how to turn any network (or testnet!) into a bitcoin miner
active_posts.summary: - The p2share network distributes mining rewards uniquely by awarding entire bitcoin rewards randomly to one shareholder.  - This model enhances fairness, efficiency, and supports a decentralized, trustless mining environment.  - Its design aims to reshape mining incentives and foster innovation within Bitcoin mining ecosystems.

active_posts.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions
active_posts.summary:
- A Bitcoin-native Language Model will enhance general LLMs' interaction with Bitcoin-specific elements.
- It requires a substantial dataset from various Bitcoin-related sources and high-quality instruction pairs.
- The model's architecture incorporates both static and live data functionalities, not for surveillance but to aid developers.

today_in_history_posts.title: bitcoin-transactions
today_in_history_posts.summary: - The email discusses risks of online services demanding private keys.  - It introduces https://peersm.com/wallet, which makes private keys optional.  - Users report issues with bech32 addresses, causing confusion post-segwit.

today_in_history_posts.title: Lack of capacity field in channel_announcement makes life difficult. Why is it not there?
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Nodes should discard erroneous messages and optimize update broadcasts.
- Christian Decker recommends nodes track an internal UTXO set for efficiency.
- Proposals include prioritizing newer channels and limiting fetched historical blocks.

today_in_history_posts.title: Bolt 12 Trusted Contacts
today_in_history_posts.summary: - Bolt 12 enhances UX by mimicking traditional payment apps, adding contact management and easier payments.  - It proposes contact key distribution and mutual authentication for selective identity sharing.  - Feedback is sought to refine methods for identity revelation in Bolt 12 payments.
